objects.name: GLSS Framework Matrix
objects.description: Ground-Link-Space Security — Unified threat modeling framework for space systems cybersecurity.

objects.name: Ground Reconnaissance
objects.description: Techniques used to gather information about ground segment infrastructure including control centers, ground stations, and supporting IT/OT systems.

objects.name: Ground Initial Access
objects.description: Techniques used to gain initial foothold in ground segment systems.

objects.name: Ground Execution
objects.description: Techniques used to execute malicious code on ground segment infrastructure.

objects.name: Ground Persistence
objects.description: Techniques used to maintain access to ground segment systems across restarts and credential changes.

objects.name: Ground Privilege Escalation
objects.description: Techniques used to gain higher-level permissions on ground segment systems, including access to mission-critical control interfaces.

objects.name: Ground Lateral Movement
objects.description: Techniques used to move through ground segment networks, pivoting from IT to OT and mission control systems.

objects.name: Ground Collection
objects.description: Techniques used to gather mission data, telemetry, and operational information from ground segment systems.

objects.name: Ground Exfiltration
objects.description: Techniques used to steal data from ground segment infrastructure.

objects.name: Ground Impact
objects.description: Techniques used to disrupt, degrade or destroy ground segment operations and mission control capabilities.

objects.name: Link Signal Reconnaissance
objects.description: Techniques used to identify and characterize communication links between ground and space segments.

objects.name: Link Signal Interception
objects.description: Techniques used to intercept and eavesdrop on communications between ground and space segments.

objects.name: Link Signal Injection
objects.description: Techniques used to inject malicious commands or data into communication links.

objects.name: Link Signal Jamming
objects.description: Techniques used to disrupt or deny communication between ground and space segments through electromagnetic interference.

objects.name: Link Signal Spoofing
objects.description: Techniques used to forge or replay legitimate signals to deceive ground or space systems.

objects.name: Link Protocol Exploitation
objects.description: Techniques exploiting vulnerabilities in space communication protocols (CCSDS, TT&C, ISL).

objects.name: Space Initial Compromise
objects.description: Techniques used to gain initial access to space segment systems including satellite bus and payload.

objects.name: Space Payload Manipulation
objects.description: Techniques used to alter, disable or hijack satellite payload operations.

objects.name: Space Firmware Tampering
objects.description: Techniques targeting onboard firmware and software including malicious OTA updates and embedded AI manipulation.

objects.name: Space Data Exfiltration
objects.description: Techniques used to extract sensitive data from space segment systems.

objects.name: Space Denial of Service
objects.description: Techniques used to disrupt or degrade satellite operations and services.

objects.name: Space Orbital Impact
objects.description: Techniques causing physical consequences in orbit including attitude control manipulation and orbital parameter modification.

objects.name: Malicious command injection on spacecraft
objects.description: Injection de commandes non autorisées ou altérées vers le satellite (via commande directe ou via relais) pour modifier la configuration, changer le pointage, perturber la charge utile ou dégrader définitivement la mission. Inclut le contournement des mécanismes d’authentification/crypto du lien de commande.

external_references.name: RF jamming of space-ground links
external_references.description: Brouillage RF ou interférences intentionnelles sur les liaisons montantes ou descendantes (commande, télémétrie, payload) pour dégrader, interrompre ou rendre inexploitables les communications entre segments sol et spatial.
